sections.paragraph:
εὐθύς adv. (developed fr. the nom. masc. sg. of εὐθύς)

① immediately, at once (so Pind.; Thu. 2, 93, 4 +; Epict.; pap [Mayser 244; also POxy 744, 7 [1 b.c.]; PRyl 234, 4]; LXX; TestSol 18:5, 37; TestAbr [παρʼ εὐθύς A 19 p. 102, 20/Stone p. 52]; Test12Patr; ParJer 5:20; Philo; Jos., Ant. 11, 236 al.; Just.; s. Phryn. 144f Lob.; WSchmid, D. Atticismus I 1887, 121; 266; II 1889, 113; in the mss. and edd., esp. t.r., freq. the form εὐθέως, q.v.) Mt 3:16; 13:20f; 14:27; 21:3 (but εὐθέως vs. 2); oft. in Mk e.g. 1:10, 12 (on the originality of the word, not the form, in Mk s. JWeiss, ZNW 11, 1910, 124–33); Lk 6:49; J 13:30, 32; 19:34; Ac 10:16; ISm 3:2; Hv 3, 13, 2; 5:2, 4; m 5, 1, 3; 11:12; Hs 7:4.

② For the inferential use, weakened to then, so then e.g. in Mk 1:21, 23, 29 s. Mlt-H. 446f.—PVannutelli, Synoptica 1, ’38, cxiv–cxxvi; GRudberg, ConNeot 9, ’44, 42–46. Mlt-Turner 229. S. on εὐθέως. DDaube, The Sudden in the Scriptures ’64, 46–72; LRydbeck, 167–176, 184.—M-M. EDNT. TW.

adv. adv. = adverb, adverbially

  1. fr. = from

masc. masc. = masculine

  1. sg. = singular

Pind Pind , V b.c.—List 5

Thu Thu , V b.c.—List 5

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

Mayser Mayser = EM., Grammatik der griechischen Papyri aus der Ptolemäerzeit—Lists 4, 6

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

PRyl PRyl = Catalogue of the Greek Papyri in the John Rylands Library—List 4

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

TestAbr TestAbr = Testament of Abraham, with some interpolations; I b.c./I a.d.—Lists 2, 5

Test12Patr Test12Patr = Testaments of the Twelve Patriarchs, with interpolations II b.c.–III a.d.—List 2

ParJer ParJer = Paraleipomena Jeremiou, II? a.d.—List 2

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

Phryn Phryn , the Atticist, signifying his Eclogae, II a.d.—List 5

Lob. Lob. = CLobeck, s. Phryn.—List 5

edd. edd. = editions, editors

esp. esp. = especially

t.r. t.r. = textus receptus

freq. freq. = frequent(ly)

q.v. q.v. = quod vide (which see, see that which has just been mentioned)

oft. oft. = often

e.g. e.g. = exempli gratia (for example)

ZNW ZNW = Zeitschrift für die ntl. Wissenschaft—List 6

ISm ISm = Ignatius to the Smyrnaeans, I–II a.d.—List 1

Hv Hv = Visions

m m = Mandates

Hs Hs = Similitudes

Mlt-H. Mlt-H. = JMoulton/WHoward, A Grammar of NT Greek II: Accidence and Word-formation—List 6

ConNeot ConNeot = Coniectanea Neotestamentica—List 6

Mlt-Turner Mlt-Turner = JMoulton/NTurner, A Grammar of NT Greek III: Syntax; IV: Style, by Turner—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

EDNT EDNT = Exegetical Dictionary of the NT—List 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 406
